reviews.title: Love this watch
reviews.text: Nice looking watch with accurate steps. I love the gps feature where it shows your walk on a true map on the app. Nice visual. Heart rate is very accurate as well. Haven't used music feature as much as I thought I would. Garmin makes a nice watch. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Heart rate way off
reviews.text:
The main thing I bought this for is to monitor heart rate while hiking. Its reading is way off for me - as confirmed by a pulse ox and manually counting. The watch kept telling me my heart rate was over 100 just around the house. I have nsvt but my heart rate generally runs low. So I'm very sensitive when I have palpitations - when this watch kept saying my heart rate was over 95 I wasnt buying it. That brings into question any other readings or parameters.

I find the watch annoying and frustrating to use. The watch face does not interact very well for me. I'm constantly trying to tap it and it wont register so then the screen shuts off. Another annoying thing is it tells you to start moving around when I'm doing housework. I guess you can shut that off but I've really lost interest. Hopefully Garmin can offer some fix for the reading since its past the return date.

The positives - watch is comfortable and I don't wear jewelry. I forget its on. Its a large size so its readable if you have vision issues. I've had no problem with that.

reviews.title: Measure your wrist before you buy.
reviews.text:
The blue silicone band was too small to go around my wrist. I called Skagen and they offered to sell me a bigger band. I told them I shouldn't have to pay extra for the watch to go on my wrist. They said since I bought it from Amazon there was nothing they could do. So sadly, this is getting returned.
I've worn plenty of watches before, and I don't have a fatty wrist. My wrist is 8 inches in circumference, which is average for a male.
The interesting thing about the size as I inspected and measured the watch, is that if you measure the watch laid out flat, 8 inches goes to the second to last notch, and it should fit fine. BUT -- the watch body itself obviously does not bend, which means in actual wear when the watch goes AROUND your wrist, you need an additional 3/4 to 1 inch for it to work.
It's basically like that one gallon jug I bought at Walmart, I got it home to make a drink mix in it just to realize that yes, it held one gallon, if you filled it to the absolute brim, at which point putting in the drink mix made it spill.
If your wrist is greater than 7 inches in circumference, I would not buy this watch.

reviews.title: It works as a watch but most other functions are suspect
reviews.text:
This smart watch looks nice enough - someone could easily confuse it for an older Apple Watch - and its easy to make custom watch faces (I put a pic I took at an Iron Maiden show on mine). And it is jam packed with features - way more than some other watches in this price range.

Unfortunately, I have trouble trusting its more advanced features.

A number of times when taking my temperature the watch says I have hypothermia, measuring me between 87-95f. I am a 200lb man living in in Southern California and my wife won’t turn the thermostat down below 74 degrees. I don’t have hypothermia. If it can’t properly read my body temperature, there’s no way I can trust its accuracy with many of the other health features - blood oxygen, blood pressure, heart rate, etc

That step count seems pretty accurate but ONLY once I paired the watch with the Da Fit app. Before I paired the watch, it measure me taking like 350 steps while just sitting on my couch. Not sure why it would measure differently paired or unpaired but it did - significantly. I would not recommend this as a fitness tracker for someone without a smart phone (yes, there are still a few people without smart phones).

And the sleep tracker seems to at least get my sleep times accurate, though I am unsure how well it reads light/deep sleep as I don’t really trust the readings from it’s health sensors. I want to believe its doing a good job. I like the way the app presents the sleep timeline and information. But I just don’t know if its accurate because the watch thinks I have hypothermia.

As for the non-health related functions, they are pretty spotty too. I stopped receiving text notifications after a couple days and could not get them to start back up again without completely disconnecting the phone from the app and starting over again.

Pros:
- Looks like an old gen Apple Watch.
- Customizable face
- Lots of features
- Doesn’t technically *need* a smart phone for many features
- Step counter seems accurate when paired
- Great battery life for a smart watch. I wore it for 5 days without charging.
- Nice app

Cons:
- Highly inaccurate step counter when it is not paired
- Health features are unreliable
- Notifications are unreliable
- The speaker in the watch sounds terrible
- Vibration function is weak and easily missable in active situations
- Sometimes the watch doesn’t ‘wake’ from sleep as easily/instantly as it should. I find myself having to give it an extra shake to wake it almost every time.

reviews.title: Awesome Smart Watch
reviews.text:
For the money, this is an awesome smart watch that has some of the same popular features as that “fruit” brand.
The watch was packaged securely in a box and included a User Manual and a “How to Connect with the smart watch” brochure.

My watch was received with 46% battery life and did not need to be charged immediately, but I noticed that the charge cord is magnetic and attaches easily to the back of the watch.

I read the instructions and powered on the watch. I downloaded the app and proceeded to pair, referred to as “bind” in the manual. The smart watch paired effortlessly with my iPhone 13. Other than pairing or unpairing, the user manual doesn’t cover much else, but I found the watch use to be intuitive. I do think a more detailed user manual would be helpful to explain navigating and swipe direction of the screens.

The watch has many functions and I was pleasantly surprised that the watch provided a “Siri connection” and performed flawlessly. I compared the blood pressure and oxygen sensors to my individual monitors to check accuracy. Surprisingly, the watch is pretty accurate.

As I mentioned earlier, I started using the watch with 46% battery life and wore the watch for approximately 25 hours before I received a low battery alert.

This is a great buy and so much so, that within hours of using my watch, I ordered another one for my husband.

reviews.title: Easy to set up, very accurate, keeps me updated with many aspect of my health
reviews.text:
I really appreciate this watch and the features it has. First off, the smart watch was very easy to pair to my phone. I have an Android phone, and it only took a few minutes to pair the watch with my phone.
First enable Bluetooth and find the device listed as "C20"

Next, download the app that's shown in the QR code. Once you have the watch paired via Bluetooth to your smart phone and the app is downloaded, your watch ready to go, and should be syncing to your smart phone. The connection has been great thus far; it hasn't disconnected/lost connection to my phone.

As someone who has suffers from pulmonary lung disease, I was very excited when I found out this smart watch has the capability to track my oxygen saturation, heart rate, and blood pressure. In addition, you don't have to do anything, the watch will automatically to measurements periodically throughout the day.

It's helping me monitor and compare results over a few days. I suggest authorizing Google Fit so your hearth history is accessible when it. For me, it helps to have a history when I meet with my pulmonologist.

The watch has great standby battery life, and it's light weight. After m three days it's only dipped minimally in available battery percentage. I highly recommend this!

**Updated**

So it's been several days now since I first posted the review and I am amazed how little the battery has drained. Needless to say this smartwatch has fantastic standby battery life; especially considering it's constantly looking for/measuring heart rate. As you can see from the photos the readings are very accurate. I own a pulse oximeter and a blood pressure monitor to keep my pulmonary hypertension in check. My resting heart rate is 95 bpm, which may seem high, but the reading is accurate and normal which makes this product more the reassuring.

reviews.title: Great Watch!
reviews.text: Ok, I have had fitness watches from Samsung to Fitbit to Garmin and this one is the best....especially for the price.  First thing since turning 60 I need a watch I can read in the sunlight.  This has a brightness level I actually do not have to turn it all the way up and the workout data is big enough to read. I think all the health data is correct, but do not trust the Blood Pressure readings because of the science behind it. Actually do not trust any BP on a fitness watch.  There is a lot of customization you can do......I recommend a custom watch face along with setting up quick view on a schedule. I am now going to see if I can get a spare charging cord,

reviews.title: Cheap, Inaccurate, but Cheap
reviews.text:
What works, the clock.
What doesn't work, everything else.
Bought in August, really tried to give it a chance. I knew for the price it was a gamble.

reviews.title: Step count
reviews.text:
Like the look, heft, easy set up.

But I question the accuracy since driving 1 miles and it's counted 30 steps, this is AFTER it logged 82 steps during at my desk.

3 month update. Battery life is still excellent however the watch has stopped connecting to the app

6 month update: Batter life is excellent, I notice it has started to decline, but that could also be heavier use. Step count still seems a bit wonky, but not egregiously off. The Strap is starting to break at the clasp and I will need to replace as soon as I'm able to find a replacement.

reviews.title: Nice

reviews.title: Great watch for a great price
reviews.text: I was skeptical about the watch as I had purchased other watches only to have them fail miserably. This watch turned out great. The most important thing to do is read the manual. Especially about how to wear the watch, which is a little higher than a normal watch. This will make the accuracy much better. The step counter is controlled by arm movement not actual steps, so if you walk stiff armed , it will be inaccurate. Move your arms when walking , the watch will be much more accurate.Make sure you go into the app on your phone and activate the exercises your interested in. This will help the watch to track it better. Also activate the media part of the app for sms,email etc. You can also monitor who is calling you, so you can choose to answer you phone or not. I am extremely pleased with my purchase and will be watching for other products from this company.

reviews.title: Didn't count steps
reviews.text: I used this for a few weeks because I didn't feel my band was counting steps when I was shopping or carrying something.  I was very surprised to see that after a full day out-and-about I had under 300 steps on the counter.  That's roughly what I would normally have when I got up to get my morning coffee.  I tried re-adjusting and tried the other leg, but got the same result.  I'm back to keeping my tracker on my wrist.  Disappointed.

title: Apple Watch Series 6 (GPS + Cellular, 40mm) - Graphite Stainless Steel Case with Black Sport Band (Renewed)
description: Monitor even more of your health with the Apple Watch Series 6. The Series 6 adds pulse oximetry to measure your blood's oxygen levels (SpO2) in just 15 seconds, as well as showing you your VO2 Max (maximum oxygen consumption) score. These indicators can provide early warnings to possible respiratory conditions. They are joined by an optical heart rate monitor and the ability to record your own ECG (electrocardiogram). The Series 6 can even detect and help you with healthy habits, such as hand-washing detection, which displays a 20-second animated countdown as you scrub. Fitness is a large part of staying healthy, and the Apple Watch has a variety of ways to assist you in reaching your goals. Activity Rings displays three rings that correspond to movement, exercise, and standing. Stay mobile throughout the day to hit new persol bests as you close the rings. You can also choose from a variety of workouts, such as running, swimming, yoga, cycling, and more; with the watch recording and tracking their specific metrics.Fitness and health metrics can be viewed, customized, and tracked on the Apple Watch's curved watch face that houses an always-on LTPO OLED Reti display. Even in direct sunlight you can view the screen with just a glance. As you move, choose from a variety of music, podcasts, and audiobooks to entertain and motivate you. For added fun, share your activity rings with friends and challenge them to 7-day activity competitions. Throughout the day, the Apple Watch Series 6 keeps you connected with the world around you. When paired with your iPhone, you can make calls and send texts directly from your wrist, vigate your surroundings with Maps, buy items with Apple Pay, and use your voice to interact with Siri. Made to last in almost any kind of weather, the Apple Watch Series 6 is water-resistant up to 164'. Use its 32GB of built-in storage for apps, watch faces, music, and more.
